markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create fixtures for stale manual detection, missing page, legacy name redirect, search, context bundle citation, and visual navigation. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualFixtures leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualFixtures is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: UserManual negative-path fixtures are implemented and exercised for stale, missing, alias, search, context citation, and orphan navigation cases; proof and adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: UserManual fixture helpers and tests cover stale hash tampering, missing page deletion and reseed healing, unknown legacy alias, literal bounded search, context-bundle citation, and orphan page navigation detection.
MT-208 targeted content tests passed against real PostgreSQL; broader API/storage suites cover stale hash and literal bounded search fixtures.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.
Heisenberg read-only adversarial review returned PASS for MT-208. Non-blocking hardening gap: same-count child-row tampering is not covered by current freshness fixtures.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-208 requires negative-path UserManual fixtures (stale detection, missing page, legacy-name redirect, bounded search, context-bundle citation, orphan navigation) proving the manual surface cannot false-PASS. The code delivers all six families as real PostgreSQL operations, driven to their negative verdicts by PG-backed tests over the Handshake-native store/freshness/bundle/API path, with no mocks, placeholders, or deferred escapes.
findings: All six MT-208 fixture families are real SQL against PostgreSQL in src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/fixtures.rs: tamper/restore content hash (L27-57), delete_page cascade (L60-66), insert_orphan_page (L70-91), unreachable_pages BFS reachability audit over stored rows (L97-136). No mock store.
Stale fixture is driven through the live HTTP /usermanual/freshness route and asserts stale_content for the tampered page, then heals on restore: tests/user_manual_api_tests.rs L536-571.
Missing-page fixture deletes a seeded page, asserts FreshnessVerdictKind::MissingPage, then reseeds and confirms recovery: tests/user_manual_content_tests.rs L678-711.
Legacy-redirect fixture asserts a known alias resolves to its canonical route and an unknown alias returns None (no fuzzy resolution): tests/user_manual_content_tests.rs L716-737.

Bounded/literal search fixture asserts a seeded term hits, a nonsense term is empty, and '%' is treated literally (wildcard escaped): tests/user_manual_storage_tests.rs L613-638; escaping confirmed in store.rs search() L609-618 (.replace('%','backslash-percent')).

Citation fixture compiles a real ContextBundle and reloads it from PG, asserting the persisted citation carries slug+version+section-anchor+content-hash-prefix: tests/user_manual_content_tests.rs L355-432.
Orphan-navigation fixture asserts the seeded corpus has no orphans, then inserts an orphan and asserts the audit flags it: tests/user_manual_content_tests.rs L742-756 and api L632-634.

Test harness is real-PG-only: tests/user_manual_support.rs builds a real AppState pool on the isolated schema (L96-118) and skip_if_no_pg! PANICS when PG is absent (L132-146), so fixtures cannot silently skip; NoopLlm/NoopRecorder are only for unrelated AppState deps, not the manual store under test.

Spec-Realism gate: no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/placeholder/mock/'pending MT-' hits in fixtures.rs, freshness.rs, or store.rs (grep clean). FreshnessVerdictKind::StaleContent/MissingPage are concrete enum variants in freshness.rs L41-61.

Spec anchors (10.15.8 UserManual migration bridge, 2.3.13.11 UserManualRecord, 12.7) resolve in current bundle master-spec-v02.194 (SPEC_CURRENT v02.194); packet cites v02.192 anchors that map forward.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork only): the MT evidence references green logs under ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-009-recovery/ that are not present on disk; per review ground rules this is an evidence-paperwork concern, not a code defect, and the test code itself is real and PG-backed.
evidence_checked: tests/user_manual_api_tests.rs (stale-hash freshness HTTP, projection nav audit)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
